results.title: Dave Hansen
highlights:
Senior Accounting Executive | Public Company · REIT · SEC Reporting · Technical Accounting · AI Implementation
...
VP of Corporate Accounting at [Healthpeak Properties, Inc.](https://www.linkedin.com/company/healthpeak)
...
Over the course of my career, I've built and led accounting organizations at public companies and private equity-backed businesses, consistently raising the bar on reporting quality, process discipline, and team capability. At Healthpeak Properties, a large-cap S&P 500 healthcare REIT, I've spent the majority of my career in senior accounting leadership — most recently as VP of Corporate Accounting, where my mandate includes leading AI and efficiency implementation across the accounting function. Earlier in my tenure I served as SVP Corporate Controller, overseeing SEC reporting, technical accounting, internal controls, and the full close and consolidation process. I also played a central role in the recent Janus Living spinoff, supporting IPO readiness and SEC reporting build-out through that transaction. Before Healthpeak, I was CAO at Red Robin, where I led the accounting function through a period of significant operational change, including tightening the close cycle and strengthening the controls environment across a complex multi-unit restaurant business. What I find most meaningful in this work is developing the people around me. I take real satisfaction in building teams that are capable, independent, and trusted by the broader organization — teams that don't need the CFO or founders to solve accounting problems because they already have the leadership and judgment to handle them. My foundation includes Big Four experience at KPMG and a post-graduate technical accounting role at FASB.
...

### Integration Consultant at Healthpeak Properties, Inc.

Engaged by Healthpeak to lead accounting efficiency and systems integration initiatives during a period of significant operational change. I designed and deployed Excel-based automation tools that reduced manual processing time by more than 75%, and built strategic financial reporting packages to support a newly appointed CFO in gaining rapid visibility into key performance metrics. As the engagement evolved, I took on a central role in supporting the Janus Living spinoff IPO — including preparation of the historical financial statements included in the SEC Form S-11, ensuring accuracy, completeness, and GAAP compliance through the registration process.
...
As SVP Corporate Controller for Healthpeak Properties, an S&P 500 healthcare REIT with $2.7 billion in annual revenues, I led a team of 18 professionals across the full scope of corporate accounting — consolidations, intercompany, multi-entity reporting, SEC filings, SOX compliance, and technical accounting. During my tenure I oversaw purchase price allocations for 51 acquired properties totaling $3 billion, led all joint venture standalone audits, and reviewed partnership agreements for VIE and consolidation conclusions. I was also focused heavily on the people side of the function, growing team engagement from 70% to 85%.

As part of the Corporate Finance team, which reports directly to the CFO, I am responsible for managing various aspect of the annual operating plan, monthly corporate forecast, earnings guidance, and the earnings call preparation. My responsibilities require deep knowledge of REITs and the related accounting and reporting metrics, and the major business drivers such as real estate operations, capital spend, capital markets, tax, and G&A. Our team is responsible for overseeing reporting prepared for both the C-suite and Board of Directors My specific responsibilities include: 1) Ensuring that the corporate forecast assumptions are accurate and timely. This includes oversight and analysis of items such as Net Operating Income, secured and unsecured debt, and interest income. 2) Same-store growth reporting and analysis 3) Preparation of detailed forecast walks to explain major variance drivers in all aspects of the business 4) Transaction sensitivity analysis 5) Preparation of specific reports and analysis to prepare the C-suite for quarterly earnings calls 6) Ad-hoc financial analysis and projects that support executive decision making and process improvement I have intimate working knowledge of BPC, MRI, and various other accounting and reporting tools

### https://www.linkedin.com/posts/healthpeak_healthpeak-properties-closes-merger-with-activity-7169343684981850113-IwWA
...
We are pleased to share that our previously announced merger with Physicians Realty Trust is now completed. The combined company will operate as “Healthpeak Properties, Inc.” and is expected to begin trading under the ticker symbol “DOC” at the open of trading on The New York Stock Exchange on March 4, 2024. 
 
“We believe this transaction augments our earnings, balance sheet, and platform. Our integration efforts are progressing ahead of schedule, with property management internalized in four markets to date, with an additional five markets scheduled by the end of the second quarter. We expect to generate merger-related synergies of $40 million during 2024 with potential for $20 million or more of additional synergies by year-end 2025,” said Scott Brinker, President and Chief Executive Officer of Healthpeak.
...
In this message, our President & CEO, Scott Brinker , reflects on the strategy behind Healthpeak’s sector-leading performance and the improvements we’ve made since our merger with Physicians Realty Trust. Our results demonstrate that while strategy can be imitated, consistent execution is what truly differentiates Healthpeak in creating lasting shareholder value.

Scott Brinker , President and Chief Executive Officer, shared, “Yesterday, we announced an increase to our dividend, which is an important part of our total return to shareholders. Beginning in April, we expect to pay our dividend monthly to match the timing of our monthly rental income more closely. Our dividend payout ratio remains conservative, preserving free cash flow to reinvest into the business.”

...
Gen AI Engineer
...
The Gen AI Engineer is responsible for analyzing and modeling organizational data for the Artificial Intelligence (AI) function to draw business insights, which can be used to make business decisions.
...
- Applies data extraction, transformation and loading techniques in order to connect large data sets from a variety of sources.
...
- LLM development and fine-tuning strategies, best practices, and standards to enhance AI ML model deployment and monitoring efficiency.
...

...
- Work closely with the MLOps team to create and maintain robust evaluation solutions and tools to evaluate model performance, accuracy, consistency, reliability, during development, and UAT.
...
- Identify and implement model optimizations to
...
system efficiency.

...
### Minimum Requirements
...
- Requires a Bachelor’s degree in a highly quantitative field (Computer Science, Machine Learning, Operational Research, Statistics, Mathematics, etc.) or equivalent degree and 4 or more years of experience; or any combination of education and experience in configuration management, which would provide an equivalent background.
...
### Preferred Skills, Capabilities, and Experiences
...
- Advanced Python proficiency.
- 4+ years of professional hands-on experience leveraging large sets of structured and unstructured data to develop data-driven tactical and strategic analytics and insights using ML, NLP, and computer vision solutions.
...
- Demonstrated 4+ years hands-on experience with Python, SQL, Hugging Face, TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, and Spark.
...
- Experience with GCP/AWS cloud platforms.
- Strong knowledge of and measurable hands-on experience with developing or tuning Large Language Models (LLM) and Generative AI (GAI)
...
- Experience with NLP, LLMs (extractive and generative), fine-tuning and LLM model development.
...
- Experience developing and optimizing high-quality prompts for NLP applications.
...
- LLM Infrastructure & Deployment: LLM serving platforms (vLLM, Text Generation Inference, FastAPI); Model quantization for LLMs (GPTQ, AWQ, bitsandbytes); GPU memory optimization techniques (tensor parallelism, pipeline parallelism); LLM caching strategies for inference optimization; RAG architecture design and implementation.
...
- Advanced cloud infrastructure (AWS EKS/ECS, GCP GKE, Azure AKS) knowledge.
...
- Containerization strategies for ML workloads; Canary deployments for ML models.

results.title: Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
highlights:
Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
...
# Data Scientist
...
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
...
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
...
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
...
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
...
- Applies advanced techniques including statistics
...
machine learning, simulation, and mathematical modelling
...
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
...
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
...
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into clear, actionable insights for business stakeholders, product teams, and leadership to support data driven decision making
...
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
...
### Required Qualifications:
...
- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects
...
- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python
...
- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.
...
- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
...
- Proven skills to create opportunities using machine learning or artificial intelligence
...
Optum, part of UnitedHealth Group, is a global health services and technology company revolutionizing healthcare delivery through data analytics, pharmacy benefits, and digital health solutions that connect millions to better care. Headquartered in Eden Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.

highlights:

...
-to-date AI-powered research, analyst debate audio & saved-company playlists
...
Healthpeak Properties, Inc. is a Standard & Poor's 500 company that owns, operates, and develops high-quality real estate focused on healthcare discovery and delivery in the United States. At September 30, 2025, its portfolio consisted of interests in 703 properties: 530 outpatient medical, 139 lab, 15 CCRC, and 19 other properties.
...
Healthpeak Properties has concentrated exposure to South San Francisco life-science assets, allowing it to benefit from the region's innovation-led recovery. Healthpeak enjoys industry tailwinds, including an aging population and regulatory changes that expand the pool of participants in the healthcare system. However, Healthpeak's new management team doesn't have the same operational experience or relationships as its peers, which could make it difficult to achieve the same internal growth.
...
Direct REIT competitors include Omega Healthcare Investors, LTC Properties, Medical Properties Trust, Welltower, Sabra Health Care REIT, Global Medical REIT, and Ventas. Healthpeak's diversified focus across outpatient medical, lab, and senior housing differentiates it from pure-play healthcare REITs, though it faces life science oversupply pressures similar to peer Alexandria Real Estate Equities.
...

# Senior Data Scientist - ML/AI Digital Workplace Experience
...
- We are seeking a highly skilled Senior Data Scientist to join our ML Digital Experience & Digital Workplace team. In this role, you will lead the design, development, and deployment of advanced machine learning models and Generative AI (GenAI) solutions that transform the digital workplace experience. You will work at the intersection of data science, AI innovation, and enterprise technology to deliver intelligent products and capabilities that drive productivity, automation, and enhanced user experiences across the organization. This is an opportunity to shape the future of how employees interact with digital tools and platforms by leveraging cutting-edge ML and GenAI technologies to solve complex business challenges at scale.
...
- Machine Learning & AI Development: Design, build, and deploy production-grade ML models with end-to-end pipelines and MLOps best practices to ensure scalability, reproducibility, and continuous improvement.
...
Generative AI & LLMs: Lead the design and deployment of GenAI solutions leveraging LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, and fine-tuning to build intelligent assistants, conversational agents, and knowledge retrieval tools.
...
Data Analysis & Insights: Analyze large-scale datasets using statistical methods and advanced analytical frameworks to uncover actionable patterns and measure AI/ML impact.
...
Digital Workplace & Employee Experience: Partner cross-functionally to deliver high-impact AI/ML use cases—including predictive analytics, anomaly detection, and workflow automation—across enterprise digital workplace platforms.
...
Collaboration, Technical Leadership & AI Governance: Serve as a subject matter expert in ML and GenAI, mentoring team members, driving actionable business recommendations, and contributing to responsible AI governance frameworks.
...
- 5+ years of hands-on experience delivering production-level ML/AI solutions with strong expertise in Generative AI, LLMs, RAG architectures, and prompt engineering.
...
Advanced proficiency in Python, ML/AI frameworks (scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Hugging Face, LangChain), NLP techniques, SQL, and large-scale data platforms (Databricks, Spark, BigQuery).
...
Hands-on experience with MLOps practices (model versioning, CI/CD, experiment tracking, monitoring) and cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, GCP) and their AI/ML services.
...
- Preferred Qualifications
...
- Master's degree or Ph.D. in Data Science, Computer Science, Machine Learning, Statistics, Mathematics, Computational Linguistics, or a related quantitative field.
...
Experience with vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, FAISS), AI orchestration frameworks (LangChain, LlamaIndex, Semantic Kernel), and enterprise AI platforms such as Azure OpenAI Service or Azure Machine Learning.
...
Knowledge of digital workplace technologies (Microsoft 365, ServiceNow, endpoint management) with experience in enterprise telemetry and application usage analytics.
...
Familiarity with responsible AI principles and explainability techniques (SHAP, LIME), with experience building real-time inference systems and deploying models via APIs and microservices.
...
Experience with computer vision or multi-modal AI, prior work in healthcare or enterprise IT environments, and relevant cloud AI/ML certifications (Azure AI Engineer, AWS ML Specialty, Google Professional ML Engineer).
...
- Education
- Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Machine Learning, Statistics, Mathematics, Computational Linguistics, or a related quantitative field.

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

results.title: Business Intelligence Engineer | Joblume
highlights: the application in full. Below, you’ll find other important information about this position. Come join Peak Health – a fast-growing provider led health plan, as a Business Intelligence Engineer (Operational Analytics). Your data management and analysis skills combined with your critical thinking will help us advance operational performance across Peak Health by transforming raw data into actionable intelligence that improves efficiency, throughput, quality, and member/provider experience. The Business Intelligence Engineer will collaborate closely with the Operational Analytics team, as well as peers and colleagues, across product, operations, medical management, and provider contracting to support and implement high-quality, data-driven decisions. The ideal candidate has a knack for seeing solutions in data sets and the business mindset to convert insights into strategic opportunities. They will ensure data accuracy and consistent reporting by designing and creating optimal processes and procedures for analytics employees to follow. They will use advanced data modeling, predictive modeling, and analytical techniques to interpret key findings from company data and leverage these insights that will help us design, build, and manage our health plan. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: EDUCATION, CERTIFICATION, AND/OR LICENSURE: 1. Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Mathematics, Computer Science, Statistics, Information Systems, or a related field. EXPERIENCE: 1. Two (2) years of experience monitoring, managing, manipulating, and drawing insights from data in a managed care company. 2. Two (2) years of experience working with claims data to evaluate reimbursement changes, payment discrepancies, medical expense opportunities, quality outcomes and risk. PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS: EDUCATION, CERTIFICATION, AND/OR LICENSURE: 1. Two (2) years of experience working with Medicare and/or Medicaid Data. 2. Epic Accreditation for Cogito, Caboodle Data Model, and Clarity Data Model or able to obtain within 60 days of hire EXPERIENCE: 1. Familiarity with health plan operations, data architecture, and Medicare/Medicare regulatory environments. CORE DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: The statements described here are intended to describe the general nature of work being performed by people assigned to this position. They are not intended to be constructed as an all-inclusive list of all responsibilities and duties. Other duties may be assigned. 1. Assists with the development, scaling, improvement, and maintenance of data models, dashboards, and analytical solutions supporting Peak Health operations 2. Leverages complex analytical tools to provide business and technical expertise to create data driven solutions. 3. Partners with product, operations, medical management, and provider contracting teams to understand & anticipate reporting requirements, priorities, and key performance indicators. 4. Supports key business stakeholders, translating data into actionable insights using appropriate analytical solutions. 5. Produces repeatable and ad-hoc reports for key business stakeholders to measure and interpret business trends. 6. Participates in a variety of assigned operations initiatives to ensure success in meeting business goals and objectives. 7. Collects report requirements – interviews and researches business stakeholder needs; drafts, edits and validates requirement documentation. 8. Provides input on project plans, scope of work, status, and project summary reports. 9. Communicates results of analysis effectively and regularly to business stakeholders. 10. Other duties as assigned. PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. 1. Working at a computer. WORKING ENVIRONMENT: The work environment

This role is ideal for candidates with a strong foundation in data analysis, machine learning, statistical modeling, and Generative AI applications. The Associate Data Scientist will work closely with cross-functional teams to analyze large datasets, develop predictive models, generate actionable insights, and support data-driven business decisions.
